Petition To Remove Trustee Denise Rooke And Appoint A Neutral Court-Supervised Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Continued To Sep-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for and with Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Woodford G. Rowland for David Colyer and the Johanna Colyer Estate (via Zoom).
William Bailey appeared in person (pro per).


The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than August 19, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 26, 2026. The matter is continued to September 2, 2026 and the Parties are to return on September 2, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-02-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for and with Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Woodford G. Rowland for David Colyer and the Johanna Colyer Estate (via Zoom).
William Bailey appeared in person (pro per).

Mr. Bailey stated that he circulated a copy of the supplement to parties, that he plans on hiring a civil rights attorney. The matter is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ryan Kent and Victoria Shaw for Arthur Bailey, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Woodford G. Rowland for David Colyer and the Johanna Colyer Estate (via Zoom).
William Bailey appeared in person (pro per).

The Court heard no oppositions to the Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record. Mr. Bailey stated that he will retain counsel to assist him. The Court granted the motion.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

Petition For Order Compelling Trust Accounting And Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Continued To Sep-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Kent and Victoria Shaw for Arthur Bailey, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Woodford G. Rowland for David Colyer and the Johanna Colyer Estate (via Zoom).

The court acknowledged the joint status report filed April 15, 2025. Mr. Kent requested a further status conference and argued that continued court supervision is necessary to keep things moving along. Dr. Gomez objected, arguing that monthly hearings are unnecessary and burdensome.

Discussion was held regarding the progress of property listings, accounting amendments, and a recent buyout proposal. Mr. Rowland indicated a buyout offer was submitted that morning, but sale may proceed if it is not accepted.

After discussion, the court agreed that continued supervision is warranted. The court continued the matter to September 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Counsel was directed to file a further joint status report no later than August 20, 2025.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Compelling Trust Accounting And Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Continued To Apr-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan Kent and Victoria Shaw for Arthur Bailey (Petitioner) and William Bailey (Guardian Ad Litem for Petitioner).
William Bailey was also present in court.
Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for and with Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Woodford G. Rowland for David Colyer and the Johanna Colyer Estate (via Zoom).

The court noted receipt of the status reports filed by counsel. Mr. Kent stated that the submission from Dr. Gomez raised additional concerns regarding the lack of a dedicated trust account for certain assets and conflicting claims over the Citibank account which was initially identified as a trust asset but is now claimed as part of Johanna Collier's estate. Mr. Kent suggested that the court set a briefing schedule as to the entitlement to the funds in the account. Mr. Rowland suggested resolving the matter through further factual research and discussion.

Dr. Gomez stated that the Citibank account remains blocked with approximately $34,000, ensuring no dissipation of funds. She reported that a trust bank account is being established and a more detailed plan of distribution can be filed after resolving potential tax issues.

The court ordered the parties to meet and confer before any briefing is scheduled. The parties were directed to meet and confer via Zoom or in-person no later than April 8, 2025. A joint status report attesting to the results of the meet and confer shall be filed by April 15, 2025. Dr. Gomez was instructed to take the lead in preparing this report. The court continued the matter to April 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Compelling Trust Accounting And Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Continued To Mar-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan A. Kent and Victoria Shaw for Arthur Bailey, Petitioner.
Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for and With Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
William Bailey, Brother to Petitioner.

Discussion was held regarding declarations recently filed providing status updates from counsel. Dr. Gomez argued that income accumulated before Johanna Bailey's passing and should not be subject to further review. Mr. Kent argued that since Ms. Bailey was incapacitated as early as May 2021 (when a conservatorship petition was filed), there was concern that funds were mismanaged or used improperly.

The court continued the matter to March 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and ordered the parties to meet and confer on the disputed accounting issues and document production.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Compelling Trust Accounting And Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Continued To Feb-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ryan A. Kent for Arthur Bailey, Petitioner/Beneficiary.
Dr. Johanna T. Gomez for and with Denise Rooke, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
William Bailey, Brother to Petitioner.

Dr. Gomez requested 120 days to complete the accounting due to issues such as appraiser availability and property management challenges. Mr. Kent raised concerns about delays in providing information regarding the trust and requested a deadline of January 31, 2025.

The court expressed concern over the delay in responding to information requests dating back to June 2024 and directed Ms. Rooke, through her counsel, to provide all requested information to petitioner's counsel by January 31, 2025.

The court continued the matter to February 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for further updates.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Exchange Trust Property

Petition To Exchange Trust Property
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Continued To Dec-24-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Exchange Trust Property
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ian Sammis for Adam Gensleri (Petitioner). Paul Melbostad for William Bailey.

Mr. Sammis requested the petition be granted subject to the filing of the hold harmless declarations of the Grandchildren stating that he was waiting for the last two, and reported that two private investigators attempted to locate Arthur Bailey and were unsuccessful. The Court asked if he had followed up with the Social Security office in Sonoma County. Mr. Sammis stated that the Social Security department would not release any information. The Court dispensed with notice to Arthur Bailey and continued the matter to December 10, 2007. No appearance is necessary if the hold harmless agreements are filed before then.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mizota.

Petition To Exchange Trust Property
Off Calendar Oct-16-2007 Continued To Nov-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Ian Sammis appeared for Johanna Colyer (Petitioner). Paul Melbostad specially appeared for William Bailey.

Mr. Sammis requested dispensing with notice to Arthur Bailey stating that due diligence and publication of notice was done. The Court stated that the grandchildren had no rights until the daughter is deceased. Mr. Samis argued that the daughter is the trustee and this is her petition. The Court expressed concern about William Bailey signing the consent with contigencies for a buy out due the fact that Arthur Bailey is missing. Mr. Melbostad stated that William Bailey did not initiate the buy out, that he was brought into the matter to negotiate the buy out amount and that his client needed the money to purchase a home in Mill Valley. The Court instructed Mr. Samis to: 1)continue efforts to locate Arthur Bailey 2)to have each of the grandchildren execute a hold harmless to the trustee if they are to pay off one grandchild. Mr. Melbostad represented that the value of his client's gift is being dicsounted and his client will be injured if the buy out does not go forward. The Court continued the matter to November 19, 2007 and directed Counsel to file their briefs regarding Cy-Pres doctrine on how to best carry out the Grantor's intentions.

Judge: Herbert Donaldson. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth CSR#5162. Investigators: Manuel Valenzuela and Cynthia Webb-Beckford. Bailiff: O. Arias. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition To Exchange Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-11-2007 Continued To Oct-16-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Ian Sammis appeared.
Mr Sammis has a copy of the judgment from a prior case that provided for the distribution. He reported that Wiliiam Bailey has a stipulation and it is being worked on. He also reported that the appointment of Guardian Ad Litem is not necessary because this is an exchange of porperty and it would not affect the beneficiaries. He requseted a continuance to finish the paper work. The Court continued the matter to 10/16/2007. The Court advised counsel to cure any notice issues that exist.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera
